#1Elk Solar — Grid disturbance / utility event tripped inverters (not equipment failure) High confidence OPEN5000 kW at risk
  • [alert 06-11 09:49] Inverter - 01.13: Sungrow Inverter Fault Alert — Islanding (Grid power outage) (OPEN)
  • [alert 06-11 09:50] Inverter - 01.11: Sungrow Inverter Fault Alert — Islanding (Grid power outage) (OPEN)
  • [alert 06-13 10:06] Inverter - 01.08: Sungrow Inverter Fault Alert — Islanding (Grid power outage) (OPEN)
  • [pattern] 3 inverters faulted within 30 min of each other
  • [AI summary] “…Inverter 01.02 has been offline since May 27 at 3:29 AM EDT (forced outage, ~750 kWh/day downtime loss on sampl…”
→ Confirm utility outage/voltage event for the window; check recloser/breaker closed; verify inverters auto-restarted (production back to expected). Truck roll only if any unit failed to reconnect.
#2Sunflower Solar — Transformer over-temperature fault High confidence OPEN4998 kW at risk
  • [alert 05-17 11:37] DIGITAL I/O - XFMR ALARMS (IO2): Transformer Monitor — 90 degree liquid temp fault - Temperature greater than 90 C. (OPEN)
  • [alert 06-24 16:47] DIGITAL I/O - XFMR ALARMS (IO1): Transformer Monitor — 90 degree liquid temp fault - Temperature greater than 90 C. 105 degree liquid temp fault (OPEN)
  • [live power] site output now: -20.5 kW (capacity 9996 kW)
→ Verify cooling (radiators/fans), oil level and load profile. Sustained >90°C liquid temp degrades insulation — consider derate until inspected.

C & B Graham Energy 35 inverters2501h fault
AI Site Summary
Site producing through evening with light rain; meter shows 22,830 kWh today across METER 2 and METER 3. Several inverters are confirmed offline based on hourly production: INV-4 (since Jun 9 ~1 PM EDT, forced outage — Shutdown Command), INV-7 and INV-9 (since Jun 2, forced outages), INV-13 (since May 21), INV-17 (since Apr 22), INV-22 (Jun 9 ~5 PM, arc fault), INV-23 (since Apr 10), INV-35 (since Apr 6). INV-14 tripped Jun 9 7:21 AM (hardware self-test) but resumed. Combined ~1.4 MW offline; today's loss ~6–8 MWh vs. ~61 MWh daily estimate. 30-day production totals 740 MWh vs. ~1,782 MWh estimate (42%), reflecting cumulative inverter outages and rainy periods; EPI and availability are uncomputable due to weather station data issues. METER 1 not reporting since Apr 27; meter-to-inve

Eagle 24 inverters158h fault
AI Site Summary
All 24 CPS SCH125kTL inverters are online and producing in line with peers as of Jun 9 at 8:06 PM EDT, with current output tapering normally near sunset under light rain. Inv-8 shows a persistent ~25% deficit vs. peers (capped near 75 kW during midday hours) visible across recent days in the production chart, coincident with an active External Fan Err fault triggered Jun 9 at 3:42 PM. Classify as partial capability on ~125 kW (~3% of site); ongoing impact ~150 kWh/day. 30-day EPI averages 0.97 with availability at 99%. No major losses identified on the sampled inverter. Meter-to-inverter ratio is 0.99 — normal. Rule tool reports stale comm/weather flags from earlier in the day; hardware table shows all devices uploading within minutes — disregard as stale. Forecast: rain and light

Monroe Landfill 27 inverters155h fault
AI Site Summary
Inverter 20 has been offline since May 30, confirmed by an Insulation Resistance Low (IsolationErr) fault and stuck-at-zero AC telemetry. Classified as a forced outage affecting ~73 kW (~3.7% of site capacity), with ~2.3 MWh lost over 11 days (~210 kWh/day expected per the PV model losses for the sampled inverter). All other 26 inverters are online and producing in line with peers as of Jun 9 at 8:03 PM EDT. 30-day EPI averages 1.18 and inverter availability is 96%, the latter dragged by INV-20. Sustained EPI above 1.1 and rule tool high-energy-ratio flags across all inverters suggest a PV model or weather station calibration issue. Meter-to-inverter ratio is 0.99 (normal). Default alerts not set on the Elkor meter. Currently clear, 72°F. Light rain forecast Jun 10–11, rain Jun 12; m
Monroe Landfill — Ground fault tripping inverter offline Medium confidence OPEN128 kW at risk
  • [alert 06-23 10:30] Solectria PVI 60KTL Inverter - 20: .Chint / Solectria / Canadian String Inv Faults — Insulation Resistance Low - IsolationErr (OPEN)
  • [alert 06-24 10:31] Solectria PVI 60KTL Inverter - 22: .Chint / Solectria / Canadian String Inv Faults — Insulation Resistance Low - IsolationErr (4.0h, resolved)
  • [alert 06-17 10:31] Solectria PVI 60KTL Inverter - 20: .Chint / Solectria / Canadian String Inv Faults — Insulation Resistance Low - IsolationErr (23.0h, resolved)
  • [AI summary] “…Inverter 20 has been offline since May 30, confirmed by an Insulation Resistance Low (IsolationErr) fault an…”
→ Do NOT blind-reset. Dispatch electrician: insulation-resistance (megger) test the DC strings, inspect combiner boxes/wiring for water ingress or rodent damage, then clear GFDI and re-energize.
Monroe Landfill — Production meter / CT measurement issue — revenue data unreliable Medium confidence monitoring impact
  • [alert 06-18 09:43] Elkor Production Meter: Power Meter Check 2.0 — Power phase error A = -0.7 kW, B = -0.9 kW, C = 0.2 kW (0.4h, resolved)
  • [alert 06-18 23:25] Elkor Production Meter: Power Meter Check 2.0 — Current mismatch A = 85.4 Amps, B = 85.4 Amps, C = 99.7 Amps (0.4h, resolved)
  • [alert 06-19 00:22] Elkor Production Meter: Power Meter Check 2.0 — Power phase error A = 1.4 kW, B = -1.4 kW, C = 4.3 kW (0.4h, resolved)
  • [rule tool] 1 performance check(s) FAILING (weather: Sunny)
→ Compare meter kW vs sum of inverter kW. Phase reading ~0 on one leg → loose CT or wiring. Fix before settlement period closes; backfill from inverter data if needed.

Marble Solar 1 inverters54h fault
AI Site Summary
Marble Solar is operating normally as of Jun 9 at 5:09 PM PDT. All hardware is communicating, and Inverter 1 produced about 2.6 MW peak earlier today under clear skies and 61°F temperatures. 30-day Energy Performance Index averages 0.99 with inverter availability at 100%. Total production of 758 MWh is 107% of estimate. No major losses identified in the PV model for the sampled inverter; tracker losses are minimal (under 26 kWh/day). Meter-to-inverter ratio is 0.99, within normal range. No active alerts or monitoring flags. Forecast shows light rain Jun 10, then clear and warm (75–77°F) Jun 11–12 — favorable production conditions ahead.[DONE]

Bulloch 1B 24 inverters7h comm
AI Site Summary
All 24 inverters online and reporting as of Jun 9 at 8:07 PM EDT, with the site producing 24,000 kWh today against a 24,475 kWh estimate. Current conditions are clear and 82°F at sunset, so production is finished for the day. 30-day Energy Performance Index averages 0.99 with inverter availability at 100% over the last 10 days. PV model losses on the sampled inverter show no major issues; tracker lost energy averages ~76 kWh/day, minor. Meter-to-inverter ratio is 0.98, normal. The rule tool flagged three trackers stuck at fixed angles (Position A1 S 22, S 54, S 79) and an average error of 6.2° on S 46 — worth field verification but low energy impact. Forecast is clear to light rain with highs reaching 97°F by Jun 12; monitor for possible high-temperature derating midweek.[DONE]

Longleaf Pine Solar, LLC 40 invertershealthy
AI Site Summary
All 40 inverters are online and producing as of Jun 9 at 8:10 PM EDT, with site output tracking irradiance on a light-rain day per the production and expected chart. A site-wide outage occurred Jun 3–4, with zero meter and inverter production both days visible on the production meter chart. Cause is undetermined from available data. Estimated loss ~83 MWh (2 days, 100% capacity, ~42 MWh/day expected). Production fully recovered Jun 5. A similar full-site outage occurred May 17–19, contributing to the EPI dips on those dates. 30-day EPI averages 1.00 excluding outage days; inverter availability is 93%, dragged by the Jun 3–4 event. Meter-to-inverter ratio is 0.98 (normal). Tracker Controller 1 has been offline since Jun 3 with minimal energy impact (~16–30 kWh/day site-wide); TC
